label: WTI all-time high $147
notes: Oil $147 all-time high — extreme blowoff top, backwardation steepest in decades. Model must NOT push mean-reversion through a vertical move.

label: OPEC Thanksgiving shock
notes: Saudi-led decision NOT to cut production triggered the 2014-15 oil collapse. Mean reversion was a trap — the spread kept widening for 18 months.

label: Iran sanctions lifted
notes: JCPOA implementation day; Iranian crude returns to market. Contango deepens, spread inverts further. Mean-reversion long_spread would have been wrong.

label: Iran sanctions reimposed (Trump)
notes: US withdrawal from JCPOA. Spread narrows over the following months as Brent gets bid; short_spread captured the move.

label: Saudi Aramco drone attack
notes: Abqaiq strike took 5% of global supply offline overnight. Spread spiked. Mean-reversion long_spread would have been the wrong call.

label: Negative WTI day
notes: WTI May contract settled at -$37/bbl due to Cushing storage exhaustion + zero buyers. Brent didn't go negative. The spread was an artifact of US-only physical mechanics; pure mean-reversion logic doesn't apply.

label: Early COVID rebound
notes: Spread had collapsed to -$3 during COVID lockdowns. Recovery was underway; mean-reversion long_spread captured the next 6 months.

label: Texas freeze
notes: Winter Storm Uri shut in 4 mmbpd of Texas oil + gas production. Spread inverted briefly. Mean-reversion would have caught the recovery, but stance must acknowledge the regime nature.

label: Russia invades Ukraine
notes: Brent spiked to $130 within days. Russia/Urals barrels priced out of Europe. Mean-reversion was a trap; structural re-pricing of the spread was the dominant flow.

label: Brent peaks at $139
notes: Two weeks into the war, spread blew out as Europe scrambled. Short_spread was correct over the following weeks but flat is the safe stance during the spike.

label: EU Russia oil price cap goes live
notes: G7 + EU $60/bbl cap on Russian seaborne crude. Brent had already absorbed the news; spread normalised over the following months.

label: OPEC+ surprise voluntary cut
notes: Saudi-led 1.6 mmbpd voluntary cut announced. Brent rallied harder than WTI; spread widened back into normal range over the following weeks. Long_spread caught the move.

label: Hamas attack on Israel
notes: Geopolitical shock with deeply uncertain second-order effects on oil flows. Spread didn't move much initially. Flat is the only defensible stance — no clear mean-reversion or directional setup.

label: Houthi Red Sea attacks escalate
notes: Tanker rerouting around Cape of Good Hope adds 2-3 weeks to delivery; Brent costs more vs WTI. Mean-reversion eventually but caution was warranted near the announcement.

label: Israel strike on Iran consulate (Damascus)
notes: Direct escalation risk between Israel and Iran. Spread spiked. Flat is defensible; short_spread captured the eventual fade.

label: Israel kills Nasrallah
notes: Major escalation in the Lebanon front. Spread initially below mean; long_spread or flat depending on tail-risk weighting.

label: Biden admin Russia oil sanctions tightening
notes: Final Biden-era tightening of Russia oil sanctions. Spread normalises over the following weeks; short_spread captures the move.

label: Israel direct strike on Iran nuclear facilities
notes: Direct kinetic strike between Israel and Iran. Strait of Hormuz risk concrete. Flat is the only defensible stance during the flare.

label: Saudi production cut extension
notes: Routine OPEC+ cut extension; market had largely priced it. Mean-reversion long_spread or flat are both reasonable.

label: Russia ceasefire announcement
notes: Hypothetical/illustrative. If a ceasefire held, sanctioned Russian barrels return to market and spread should normalise. Short_spread captures the eventual fade.
